data.title: UK's flash PMI estimates show weaker manufacturing, services recover
data.description:
• Manufacturing PMI in the United Kingdom decreased to 45.10 points in May from 45.40 points in April of 2025, a 2-month low.

  • Services PMI increased to 50.20 points in May from 49 points in April 2025.

  • Composite PMI increased to 49.40 points in May from 48.50 points in April of 2025. 

  • ETFs: (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB)
  •  
  • Currency: (GBP:USD)

data.title: Microsoft files legal action against malware Lumma Stealer
data.description:
Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) said on Wednesday that its Digital Crimes Unit filed a legal action on May 13 against Lumma Stealer, an information-stealing malware used by hundreds of cyber threat actors.

The company said Lumma steals passwords, credit cards, bank accounts, and cryptocurrency wallets and has enabled criminals to hold schools for ransom, empty bank accounts, and disrupt critical services.

Between March 16 and May 16 Microsoft identified over 394,000 Windows computers globally infected by the Luma malware, said Steven Masada, assistant general counsel, Microsoft’s Digital Crimes Unit, or DCU, in a blog post.

Microsoft said that through a court order, DCU seized and facilitated the takedown of about 2,300 malicious domains that formed the backbone of Lumma’s infrastructure.

The Department of Justice, or DOJ, simultaneously seized the central command structure for Lumma and disrupted the marketplaces where the tool was sold to other cybercriminals.  Europol’s European Cybercrime Center, or EC3, and Japan’s Cybercrime Control Center, or JC3, also facilitated the suspension of locally based Lumma infrastructure, the company added.

"The growth and resilience of Lumma Stealer highlights the broader evolution of cybercrime and underscores the need for layered defenses and industry collaboration to counter threats," said Microsoft in a separate blog post.

data.title: European markets in red as investors scan flurry of economic data, Eurozone output fall
data.description:
London (UKX)  -0.59%

France’s composite PMI hits 2-month high in May; manufacturing climbs to 28-month peak.

Germany (DAX:IND) -0.80%

The HCOB Flash Composite PMI for  Germany fell to 48.6 in May 2025 from 50.1 in April, well below forecasts of 50.4. 

France (CAC:IND)  -0.77%

Euro Area flash PMI shows drop in services, new orders fall.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) fell 0.8% on Thursday, with almost every sector in the red, as investors parsed through a flurry of economic data from the region.

The HCOB Eurozone Composite PMI fell to 49.5 in May from 50.4 in the previous month, contrasting sharply from market expectations of 50.7.

On the corporate front, Easyjet (OTCQX:EJTTF) reported a loss for the first six months of the year, but said current bookings indicate it will meet expectations for full-year profit.

Deutsche Bank analysts downgraded British telecom group BT Group (OTCPK:BTGOF).

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down less than 1 basis point at 4.59%.

Germany's 10-year yield was unchanged at 2.66%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 2 basis points at 4.78%. 

In the next session: UK PMI for May.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

Seeking Alpha Editor Khushi Singh contributed to this article.

data.title: Euro Area flash PMI shows drop in services, new orders drop
data.description:
The seasonally adjusted HCOB Flash Eurozone Composite PMI Output Index, based on approximately 85% of usual survey responses and compiled by S&P Global, dropped to 49.5 in May from 50.4 in April.

HCOB Flash Eurozone Services PMI Business Activity Index at 48.9 in May (April: 50.1), a 16-month low.

HCOB Flash Eurozone Manufacturing PMI at 49.4 (April: 49.0), a 33-month-high.

HCOB Flash Eurozone Manufacturing PMI Output Index at 51.5 (April: 51.5), an unchanged pace of growth.

“The eurozone economy just cannot seem to find its footing. Since January, the overall PMI has shown only the slightest hint of growth and in May, the private sector actually slipped into contraction. Do not blame US tariffs for this one. In fact, efforts to get ahead of those tariffs might partly explain why manufacturing has held up a bit better lately. Manufacturers have now increased production for the third straight month, and for the first time since April 2022, new orders did not decline. On the flip side, service providers, who are generally less exposed to US trade policy, except in areas like international logistics, are seeing business activity shrink for the first time since November 2024. While foreign demand for services is softening, it is the sluggish domestic demand that seems to be dragging the sector down." said  Dr. Cyrus de la Rubia, Chief Economist at Hamburg Commercial Bank.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX)

Currency: (EUR:USD)

data.title: Luminar secures $200M capital commitment
data.description:
Automotive technology company Luminar Technologies (NASDAQ:LAZR) has reached an agreement with investment fund YA II PN and an unnamed investor to raise $200M.

Under the terms, Luminar will issue $35M in stated value of convertible preferred stock to the investors at an initial closing, and may subsequently issue additional tranches of convertible preferred stock in amounts of up to $35M not more than every 60 days (or 90 days if the prior tranche was more than $25M), at a purchase price equal to 96% of the stated value of the convertible preferred stock.

The proceeds from the initial $35M issuance are expected to be used for general corporate purposes and debt retirement.

“Today’s transaction provides us with additional financial flexibility and further strengthens our balance sheet,” said Tom Fennimore, Chief Financial Officer of Luminar. “We’ve made substantial progress in extending our liquidity runway with our restructuring efforts, and the additional capital available to us under this facility provides us with another tool to realize our long-term value.”

The agreement comes amid recent leadership changes. Earlier this month, Luminar’s board replaced founder Austin Russell as CEO and chair, appointing former Nuance chairman and CEO Paul Ricci to take his place.

LAZR shares fell 6% premarket on Thursday.

Source: Press Release

data.title: Trump: Time would seem right to take Fannie Mae, Freddie Mac public
data.description:
President Donald Trump said he is "giving very serious consideration" to bringing mortgage finance firms Fannie Mae (OTCQB:FNMA) and Freddie Mac (OTCQB:FMCC) public, and will decide on the matter "in the near future."

"Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac are doing very well, throwing off a lot of CASH, and the time would seem to be right. Stay tuned," Trump posted on Truth Social.

He said he would discuss the matter with Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick, and William Pulte, director of the Federal Housing Finance Agency (FHFA).

Fannie Mae (OTCQB:FNMA) and Freddie Mac (OTCQB:FMCC) were created by Congress to expand the secondary mortgage market. They buy mortgages from private lenders, pool them, and sell them as mortgage-backed securities.

They operated as private companies till the mortgage crisis in 2008, when the U.S. government placed them under the conservatorship of the FHFA to prevent their collapse.

Trump has long called for privatizing the two companies, and his allies have reportedly weighed transferring the government's over $250B stake to a proposed sovereign wealth fund.

Fannie Mae's (OTCQB:FNMA) total assets stood at $4.35T as of March 31, and those of Freddie Mac (OTCQB:FMCC) totaled nearly $3.41BT.

data.title: Crude oil drops over 1% as OPEC+ weighs another major output increase for July
data.description:
OPEC+ members are reportedly discussing whether to agree on another super-sized production increase at their meeting on June 1, potentially making it the third consecutive month in which the group would add extra barrels to the market.

An output hike of 411,000 barrels a day for July — three times the amount initially planned — is among options under discussion, although no final agreement has yet been reached, Bloomberg reported, citing delegates.

U.S. crude futures (CL1:COM) and Brent futures (CO1:COM) fell 1.4% each to $60.68 and $63.98 a barrel, respectively.

Meanwhile, OPEC's latest monthly report showed demand growth estimates for 2025 and 2026 at 1.3M bbl/day and 1.28M bbl/day, respectively, the same outlook as the previous month, even as the cartel adds supply to the market at an accelerated pace.

Eight members of OPEC+ announced a second straight monthly increase of 411K barrels of crude per day for June earlier this month.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

data.title: Germany's manufacturing PMI rises to 33-month high in May, composite PMI hits 5-month low
data.description:
• Manufacturing PMI in Germany increased to a 33-month high of 48.80 points in May from 48.40 points in April 2025.

  • Composite PMI meanwhile decreased to a 5-month low of 48.60 points in May from 50.10 points in April 2025.

  • Services PMI in Germany decreased to 47.20 points in May from 49 points in April 2025. 

  • ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR) (DAX:IND)  

  • Currency: (EUR:USD)

data.title: Rio Tinto CEO Jakob Stausholm to step down
data.description:
• Rio Tinto (NYSE:RIO) said that chief executive Jakob Stausholm will step down from his role later in 2025 at the conclusion of a succession process.
  • Jakob Stausholm joined Rio Tinto in 2018 as executive director and CFO.
  • He was appointed chief executive in January 2021.
  • During his leadership, Stausholm is credited with resetting Rio Tinto’s strategy to align with opportunities arising from the global energy transition, as well as establishing a pathway for a decade of profitable growth. 
  • Stausholm will continue to serve as CEO and as a member of the Board of Directors until a successor is appointed. The selection process for the next chief executive is already underway.
